revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Dainichi Kofun]][[ja:大日古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Dainichi Kofun | native_name =大日古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Dainichi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Dainichi Kofun | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Dainichi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.07244444|N|131.54766667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|qid=Q502209|lt=Hōfu|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|25|42.95|N|135|21|19|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Dainichi Kofun'''|大日古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takai neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|qid=Q502209|lt=Hōfu|1=Hōfu}} in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1948.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139547|title= 大日古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Dainichi Kofun is located on a plateau at the foot of Mount Nishime overlooking northwestern part of the Hōfu Plain on the right bank of the Sabagawa River in southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. It is believed to be a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however, the presumed anterior portion has been turned into a cemetery and no archaeological evidence has been found to verify its existence. Further doubt is cast by the presumed construction period of the tumulus, which appears to date from the latter half of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period, as keyhole-shaped Kofuns generally disappeared between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. It is probable, therefore, that the tumulus is an elongated {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}} style circular tumulus orientated to the southwest, with dimensions of roughly 40 by 20 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the surface of the mound. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber similar to the burial chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|en|Iwayayama Kofun|qid=Q11474389|lt=Iwayayama Kofun|1=Iwayayama Kofun}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is the longest stone chamber in Yamaguchi Prefecture with a total length of 13.2 meters, and a hollowed-out house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of stratified {{ill|Hyaloclastite|en|Hyaloclastite|qid=Q964204|lt=Hyaloclastite|1=Hyaloclastite}} was brought to this location from the {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|qid=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa basin|1=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}} in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been found. The style of the burial chamber and the sarcophagus indicate an extremely strong connection with the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]]. It is not clear who was buried in this tumulus, but according to local legends, it is believed to the tomb of {{ill|Prince Imseong|en|Prince Imseong|qid=Q716548|lt=Prince Imseong|1=Prince Imseong}} of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] (the ancestor of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|qid=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan|1=Ōuchi clan}}) <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> <gallery> File:Dainichi Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber File:Dainichi Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Dainichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor, lookin towards entrance File:Dainichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor, looking towards burial chamber </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|qid=Q6573292|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{template-inline|Dainichi Kofun|qq=}} *[https://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10022&pid=bl Yamaguchi Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_14777.html Yamaguchi Prefecture Tourism home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}]]
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
